lifecycle_detail: Local proof implemented; public release and external validation pending.
north_star: Help open-source maintainers safely delegate real work to agents by making authority narrow, portable, inspectable, and provable at every action boundary.

beneficiary.primary: Open-source maintainers allowing AI agents to work in shared code-and-conversation workspaces.
beneficiary.job_to_be_done: Delegate useful work without granting vague ambient authority.
beneficiary.current_struggle: Identity and audit records show who acted but do not necessarily encode or enforce the exact task boundary.
beneficiary.desired_progress: Express one permit once, enforce it consistently, and retain an inspectable decision receipt.

evidence.claim: Buzz gives humans and agents independently keyed identities in one signed-event workspace.
Buzz draft NIP-OA does not enforce wall-clock expiry and requires independent freshness controls.
Portable task-level capability contracts will improve trusted agent adoption.

north_star_metric.name: Verified external authorization runs
north_star_metric.definition: A run by someone outside Jalen Studio that evaluates at least one intentionally allowed and one intentionally denied action against a real workflow and retains the receipts.
north_star_metric.formula: Count qualifying runs and unique external teams over rolling 30 days.
north_star_metric.qualifying_event: An external maintainer supplies a reproducible contract/request pair or public receipt and confirms the decisions matched intended scope.
exclusions: Duplicate unchanged runs by the same team
north_star_metric.data_source: Public conformance receipt directory or issue template
north_star_metric.target: 5 runs across at least 2 external teams by 2026-08-22
north_star_metric.gameability: Friendly users could repeat trivial fixtures or count examples with no enforcement relevance.
north_star_metric.worked_example: A maintainer runs one allowed edit and one denied production deploy through their agent proxy, publishes redacted receipts, and confirms both matched intent.

proxies.name: External maintainer interviews
proxies.why: Tests whether the problem is real before integration work.
proxies.retire_when: Three maintainers have run the CLI.

end_state.three_year: A trusted interoperability layer supported by several agent runtimes and workspace systems.
end_state.five_year: Permits, revocations, receipts, and reputation move with agents across self-hosted workspaces.
end_state.ten_year: Narrow machine-verifiable authority is a default primitive for human-agent institutions.

value_loop: Maintainer expresses a narrow readable permit.
Agent runtime evaluates each protected action.
Allowed work proceeds and denied work returns actionable reasons.
Signed workspace events retain decision context.
Real failures improve the common schema and fixtures.
moat: Public corpus of real redacted edge cases.
Trust earned through explicit limits and upstream compatibility.
trust_invariants: Unsigned events are never labeled signed.
Policy decisions are not represented as sandbox enforcement.
principles: Identity, permission, enforcement, and audit are separate layers.
Portability matters more than owning the runtime.
anti_goals: Do not build another chat UI or Buzz fork.
Do not market JSON validation as sandboxing.
Do not make stars the success metric.
Do not imply endorsement by Block or Buzz.
Do not handle transactions in the first campaign.
economics_or_sustainability: Open protocol and core, with possible future paid enforcement or compliance services only after adoption proof.
distribution_or_adoption_wedge: A runnable answer to Buzz's stated agent-scoping problem, shown through an allowed-versus-denied decision receipt.

risks.control: Keep policy decision and enforcement points explicit; gate production claims on an adapter.
Interview and onboard before expanding the schema.
risks.evidence_that_changes_call: Three maintainers report no value in portable receipts.

entry_evidence: Buzz identity and scoping gap documented.
done_when: Schema, evaluator, event template, threat model, demo, and tests pass from a clean checkout.
roadmap.disproof_condition: Meaningful work boundaries cannot be expressed without runtime-specific policy.

done_when: Three external maintainers run the demo
One upstream design conversation produces substantive feedback
roadmap.disproof_condition: Maintainers consistently see no value beyond existing runtime policy.

scope: Open one respectful Buzz design discussion
stop_conditions: Three maintainers find no portable-policy need
Any known false allow remains unresolved

disagreements: External problem validation and provider-diverse review are still pending.

change_log.change: Initial hypothesis and local proof campaign.
